name: The Glebe, Lansdowne & Dow's
description: A massive consolidated hub spanning TD Place stadium, the Rideau Canal, Ottawa South, and Dow's Lake. The epicenter for skating, padding, running the pathways, and stadium sports.

name: Westboro Village
description: The hub for boutique fitness studios, Kichesippi beach access, and the SJAM winter trail. High concentration of yoga and cycle studios.

name: Hintonburg / Wellington West
description: A mix of dance studios, martial arts dojos, and community park hubs. Very walkable with a strong 'local' fitness scene.

name: ByWard Market / Lowertown
description: The heart of the city. Includes Major's Hill Park and proximity to the Global Affairs courts. High energy and very tourist-friendly.

name: Kanata Centrum / North
description: Major recreation complexes and the South March Highlands for mountain biking/hiking. The primary hub for the West End.

name: Barrhaven Central
description: Massive community centres (Minto) and a high density of neighborhood parks designed for soccer and tennis.

name: Orléans (Shenkman/Petrie)
description: Cultural core at Shenkman Arts Centre combined with Petrie Island for volleyball and waterfront trails.

name: St. Laurent / Vanier
description: Includes the Rideau Community Hub and Richelieu Park. A key area for accessible, grassroots sports and community programs.

name: RA Centre / Riverside
description: The RA Centre is Ottawa's largest private multi-sport facility. A massive destination for everything from archery to squash.
